pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page introducing the artist and directing visitors to key sections (gallery, shop, commissions, projects).
primary_functions: Introduce the artist with a hero section, featured artworks, and a brief statement.
Provide prominent entry points to Gallery, Shop Prints, Shop Originals, Commissions, and Projects.
Highlight current or upcoming exhibitions/events with links to their details.
Offer a clear call-to-action to join the newsletter and access the 2024 lookbook via the newsletter flow.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View full gallery" or Gallery card/CTA
Click "Shop Prints" CTA
Click "Shop Originals" CTA
Click "Commissions" CTA
Click "Client projects" / "Murals" CTA
Click "About the artist" CTA
Click a featured exhibition card
Click "Join Newsletter" button in newsletter CTA

access_methods.description: Accessible via "Home" link in the main header navigation and by clicking the site logo.
Primary landing URL of the site (index.html).

pages.name: Gallery
pages.description: Portfolio gallery for browsing all artworks (regardless of sale status) with rich filtering and saving tools.
primary_functions: Browse artworks in a grid or list view with thumbnail, title, year, size, and price (if applicable).
Filter artworks by category/subject (e.g., Landscape, Cityscape, Portrait), year range, price range, size, and color palette (including a Pastel option).
Sort artworks by criteria such as Price: Low to High, Price: High to Low, Year: Newest First, Size: Small to Large.
Save artworks to Favorites or to Wishlist using dedicated buttons on each artwork card.
Open an artworks detailed page or product page when applicable (for originals/prints that are for sale).

incoming_params.description: Preselected artwork category (e.g., 'landscape', 'cityscape') used to initialize the Category filter.
Lower bound for artwork creation year (e.g., 2022 for '2022 or later').
Upper bound for artwork creation year.
Lower bound for artwork price.
Upper bound for artwork price (e.g., 300 or 200 for tasks 1 and 3).
Maximum artwork width in centimeters (e.g., 50 for task 3).
Minimum artwork width in centimeters.
Maximum artwork height in centimeters.
Minimum artwork height in centimeters.
Preselected color palette, such as 'pastel', used for the Color palette filter.
Sort key matching one of getGalleryFilterOptions.sort_options.value, e.g., 'price_low_to_high', 'size_small_to_large'.
Pagination page number for the gallery grid.

params.id: primary_product_id of clicked artwork if artwork.is_for_sale and has linked product

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an artwork card that represents a purchasable original/print.
Click header "Favorites" icon/link after adding artworks to favorites.
Click header "Wishlist" icon/link after adding artworks to wishlist.
Click the site logo in the header to return home.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Gallery" link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the "Gallery" link in the footer.
Filters and sorting are controlled via query parameters so the current gallery view is sharable.

pages.name: Shop
pages.description: Commerce-focused listing page for purchasable items, used for both prints and original artworks.
primary_functions: Display products (prints or originals) in a grid including image, title, price, and key attributes.
Support filtering by product category (e.g., cityscapes, landscapes), product type (prints vs originals via categoryId), price range, size, and other relevant options.
Provide sorting options such as Newest First, Price: Low to High, Price: High to Low, and Size-based sorting.
Allow navigation into each products detail page (product.html) via clicking image or title.
Optionally allow quick actions such as Add to Cart or Add to Wishlist directly from product tiles.

incoming_params.description: Shop category identifier, 'prints' for Shop Prints or 'originals' for Shop Originals, mapped to Product.shop_category.
Subject/category filter for products, such as 'cityscape', used for tasks like selecting Cityscapes.
Lower bound for product price.
Upper bound for product price (e.g., 1000 for task 8).
Maximum product width in centimeters.
Minimum product width in centimeters.
Maximum product height in centimeters.
Minimum product height in centimeters.
Sort key matching getShopFilterOptions.sort_options.value such as 'newest_first' or 'price_low_to_high'.
Pagination page number for the shop grid.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a product image or title to open full product details.
Click the "Cart" icon/link in the header or "View cart" after a quick add.
Click the "Wishlist" icon/link in the header after saving products.

access_methods.description: Accessible via "Shop Prints" (shop.html?categoryId=prints) and "Shop Originals" (shop.html?categoryId=originals) in the header.
Accessible via "Shop Prints" and "Shop Originals" links in the footer.
Product type and filters are controlled via query parameters including categoryId.

pages.name: Product Detail
pages.description: Detailed view for a single purchasable item (print or original artwork).
primary_functions: Present full details of the artwork/print including multiple images, title, description, year, dimensions, medium, and price.
Provide configuration controls for variants such as size options (including Large 60 x 90 cm) and frame options (e.g., Black frame).
Allow users to set the desired quantity and add the configured product to the cart.
Allow users to add or remove the item from their wishlist.
Show related artworks or prints to encourage further browsing.

incoming_params.description: Product.id of the item to display on the product detail page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View cart" or the header Cart icon after adding to cart.
Click "Back to Shop" from the product detail page.
Click header "Wishlist" link to review saved items.

access_methods.description: Accessed via links from Shop, Gallery, Favorites, Wishlist, or Home using product.html?id={productId}.

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page listing all items the user intends to purchase.
primary_functions: Display all items added to the cart with key details such as title, size, frame choice, price, and quantity.
Allow users to change quantities or remove items from the cart.
Show subtotals and overall order total, including estimated taxes/shipping if desired.
Provide controls to proceed toward checkout (even if checkout is out of scope) or continue shopping.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Continue shopping" and choose prints.
Click "Continue shopping" and choose originals.
Click "Shipping & returns" information link from the cart page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Cart" icon/link in the header on every page.
Linked from add-to-cart confirmations and mini-cart links.

pages.name: Favorites
pages.description: Collection page for artworks the user has marked as Favorites from the gallery.
primary_functions: List all artworks saved as Favorites with thumbnails and basic info.
Allow users to open each artworks gallery or product detail page from the favorites list.
Enable removal of artworks from Favorites individually or via a clear-all option.
Provide navigation back to the main gallery for further browsing.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to Gallery" CTA for more browsing.

params.id: primary_product_id linked from the selected favorite artwork (if available)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View in Shop" on a favorite that is for sale.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Favorites" icon/link in the header.
Linked from the gallery after adding items to favorites.

pages.name: Wishlist
pages.description: Collection page for artworks and products the user is considering purchasing.
primary_functions: Display all items saved to the Wishlist, including title, price, and key attributes (size/type if applicable).
Allow users to open each items product detail page to review details or configure options.
Provide controls to move items from Wishlist to Cart (by opening detail and adding) or to remove them from the Wishlist.
Offer navigation back to the Gallery or Shop pages for continued browsing and saving.

params.id: wishlist item product.id when item_type is 'product'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View details" on a wishlist product.
Click "View in gallery" on a wishlist artwork.
Click header "Cart" link after adding an item from wishlist to cart.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Wishlist" icon/link in the header.
Linked from product detail and gallery pages when users save items to wishlist.

pages.name: Commissions
pages.description: Information and request form for commissioning custom artworks.
primary_functions: Explain commission options, mediums (e.g., acrylic on canvas), rough pricing tiers, and the commissioning process.
Provide a prominent 'Request a Custom Artwork' button that scrolls or jumps to the embedded commission form.
Present a commission request form with fields for Artwork type (e.g., Portrait), Medium, Budget, Preferred completion date, and a description of the desired piece.
Collect contact information such as Name and Email and submit the commission request.
Show an inline confirmation message or acknowledgment after successful submission.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the site logo after submitting or reading commission info.
Click "View portfolio" link from the Commissions page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Commissions" link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the "Commissions" link in the footer.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Overview of the artist, background, and exhibitions, including access to events that can be saved to a visit plan.
primary_functions: Present the artists biography, statement, and studio information.
Provide an 'Exhibitions & Events' section or tab listing upcoming and past exhibitions and events.
Offer date-based filtering for exhibitions, including a 'From' date field (e.g., from 2025-07-01) and an Apply control.
Allow sorting of exhibitions by date (e.g., Date: Soonest First) and link each exhibition to its detail page.
Encourage deeper engagement with links to the Visit Plan and other key site areas.

incoming_params.description: Optional section anchor such as 'exhibitions' to auto-open the Exhibitions & Events tab.
Lower bound date (YYYY-MM-DD) for exhibitions filter, e.g., '2025-07-01' in task 5.
Upper bound date for exhibitions filter.
Exhibition status filter matching getExhibitions.status (e.g., 'upcoming', 'ongoing').
Sort key for exhibitions such as 'date_soonest_first'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an exhibition title or "Details" link.
Click "Visit Plan" link in the Exhibitions & Events section.
Click "View full portfolio" link from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "About" link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the "About the Artist" link in the footer.

pages.name: Exhibition Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single exhibition or event.
primary_functions: Display full information about the exhibition, including title, venue, address, dates, description, and any featured works.
Provide an 'Add to Visit Plan' or 'Save Event' button to store the exhibition in the users visit plan list.
Offer navigation back to the exhibitions list on the About page and to the Visit Plan page.
Optionally show related events or exhibitions to encourage further exploration.

incoming_params.description: Exhibition.id of the event to show details for.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View Visit Plan" after adding the exhibition.
Click "Back to Exhibitions" link.
Click on a featured artwork from the exhibition to explore more works.

access_methods.description: Accessed from featured exhibitions on Home, the About page list, or Visit Plan via exhibition_detail.html?exhibitionId={id}.

pages.name: Visit Plan
pages.description: Page for managing exhibitions and events the user has saved to visit.
primary_functions: List all exhibitions/events the user has added to their visit plan, including dates and locations.
Allow users to open each saved exhibitions detail page to review full information.
Enable removal of individual exhibitions from the visit plan.
Provide quick navigation back to the About page or exhibitions section to discover more events.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a saved exhibition title.
Click "Discover more exhibitions" link.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Visit Plan" link in the header navigation.
Accessible via the "Visit Plan" link in the footer.

pages.name: Projects
pages.description: Case studies/projects page showcasing client work such as murals and commercial collaborations.
primary_functions: Display a list or grid of projects with thumbnail, title, client type, year, and a short summary.
Provide filters including Client type (e.g., business_commercial) and Year (e.g., 2023) and an Apply filters control.
Allow sorting or browsing through all projects and focusing on specific types like murals.
Link each project to its dedicated detail page for more imagery and context.

incoming_params.description: Client type filter such as 'business_commercial' to show only business/commercial projects.
Year filter (e.g., 2023) for narrowing project list.
Project category such as 'mural', 'installation', etc.
Sort key for projects, e.g., 'year_desc'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a project title or thumbnail.
Click a "Start collaboration" CTA directly from a project card.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Projects" link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the "Projects / Murals" link in the footer.

pages.name: Project Detail
pages.description: Detail view for a single project or case study (e.g., mural for a business client).
primary_functions: Show comprehensive information about the project including full title, client, objectives, process, and photo gallery.
Highlight key specs such as dimensions (e.g., wall size) and materials used.
Provide a 'Work With Me' or 'Collaboration Inquiry' button that leads to the collaboration form page with the project title as a reference.
Offer links to related or similar projects to inspire further interest.

incoming_params.description: Project.id of the case study to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Work With Me" / "Collaboration Inquiry" button.
Click "Back to all projects" link.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Projects list via project_detail.html?projectId={id}.

pages.name: Collaboration Inquiry
pages.description: Form page for sending collaboration and project inquiries, optionally referencing a specific project.
primary_functions: Present a collaboration inquiry form collecting Name, Email, Project reference (e.g., which project inspired the inquiry), and a free-text message.
When opened from a specific project detail page, pre-fill or clearly display the referenced project title in the Project reference field.
Allow users to describe the desired collaboration (e.g., a similar mural for a caf with given wall dimensions) and submit the inquiry.
Show an inline confirmation or thank-you message after successful submission and guide users back to Projects or the Home page.

incoming_params.description: Optional Project.id that inspired the collaboration inquiry.
Optional project title to prefill the "Which project inspired you?" field.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to projects" after submitting or cancelling the form.
Click the logo or "Return Home" link after submission.

access_methods.description: Typically accessed from a project detail page via collaboration.html?project_reference_id={id}&project_reference_title={encodedTitle}.
May also be linked as a generic "Work With Me" contact from various pages without a project reference.

pages.name: Newsletter Signup
pages.description: Dedicated page for newsletter subscription with interest selection and email frequency preferences.
primary_functions: Provide a newsletter signup form with an Email field and checkboxes for interests such as 'New Prints & Shop Updates' and 'Exhibitions & Events'.
Offer radio buttons or similar controls for selecting preferred email frequency, including a 'Monthly summary' option.
Submit the newsletter subscription and redirect the user to the newsletter thank-you page.
Offer basic reassurance about privacy and how often emails will be sent.

incoming_params.description: Optional source indicator such as 'footer_form' or 'inline_home', used to pass into subscribeToNewsletter.source.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Successful submission of the newsletter form.
Click the site logo from the signup page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Newsletter" link in the footer.
Accessible from the Home page "Join Newsletter" / "Stay Updated" buttons.

pages.name: Newsletter Thank You
pages.description: Confirmation page shown after successful newsletter signup, including the 2024 lookbook download.
primary_functions: Confirm that the newsletter subscription was successful and thank the user for signing up.
Provide a clear button or link labeled 'Download 2024 Lookbook (PDF)' that initiates the PDF download.
Offer navigational links back to key areas such as the Gallery, Shop, or Home page.
Optionally remind the user about what type of content they will receive via the newsletter.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Browse the gallery" link after subscribing.
Click "Shop new prints" link.
Click "Return Home" or the logo.

access_methods.description: Reached after successful newsletter signup form submission from newsletter.html.

pages.name: Info & Policies
pages.description: Information hub for shop-related details and site policies.
primary_functions: Explain practical information such as shipping options, lead times for framed prints, and care instructions for artworks.
Outline store policies including returns, cancellations, and damage handling.
Present a concise privacy and cookies overview along with basic terms of use for the site.
Answer frequently asked questions to reduce friction around purchasing and commissioning.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Review cart" from within shipping or returns FAQ sections.
Click a link about custom work that leads to commission details.
Click "Stay updated" CTA related to policy or updates.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Info & Policies" link in the footer.

header_links.description: Homepage with featured artworks and main navigation entry points
Browse the full artwork portfolio with filters and favorites
View and purchase print editions of artworks
Learn about and request custom commissioned artworks
Explore client projects and case studies such as murals
Read about the artist and view exhibitions and events
View exhibitions and events you have saved to visit
See artworks you have marked as favorites
See artworks and products saved to your wishlist
Review and manage items currently in your shopping cart

footer_links.description: Commercial projects and mural case studies
Your saved exhibitions and events to visit
Join the newsletter and later access the 2024 lookbook
Shipping, returns, privacy, and site usage policies
